# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Node.js Action

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  build-and-test:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Cache node modules
        uses: actions/cache@v1
        id: cache-node-modules
        with:
          path: ~/.npm
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-modules-${{ hashFiles('package*.json') }}

      - name: Install dependencies using yarn
        run: |
          npm config set cache ~/.npm
          yarn install --non-interactive

      - name: Cache pip packages
        uses: actions/cache@v1
        id: cache-pip-packages
        with:
          path: ~/.pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-packages-${{ hashFiles('requirements*.txt') }}

      - name: Install dependencies using pip
        run: |
          pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Cache configuration files
        uses: actions/cache@v1
        id: cache-config-files
        with:
          path: .env
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-config-${{ hashFiles('.env') }}

      - name: Build and test
        run: |
          yarn build
          yarn test

      - name: Deploy to production (optional)
        if: github.event.name == 'push' && github.event.branch == 'main'
        run: |
          yarn deploy
